record.field: Dolibarr is a modular open-source ERP and CRM suite that lets small and mid-sized organizations run their business from a single self-hosted web application.

Dolibarr is a modular open-source ERP and CRM suite that lets small and mid-sized organizations run their business from a single self-hosted web application.

Written in PHP, Dolibarr unifies contacts, sales, invoicing, accounting, inventory, projects and HR into one coherent system where you enable only the modules you actually need. Its lightweight, hook-and-trigger architecture and an unusually smooth upgrade path across major versions make it a practical choice for freelancers, foundations and growing companies that want full control over their data without enterprise complexity.

Key features
  • CRM with leads, prospects, opportunities and a shared agenda
  • Sales cycle: quotes, customer and supplier orders, invoices and payments
  • Double-entry accounting, bank reconciliation and tax/VAT reporting
  • Stock and warehouse management, products and services, simple manufacturing
  • Modular design (activate only the modules you need) with a large third-party marketplace
  • REST API, document management (ECM/DMS), and a built-in WAF, CSRF and HTTPS hardening
Best for

Freelancers, small and mid-sized businesses, associations and foundations that want a self-hosted, all-in-one ERP/CRM they can grow into module by module while keeping their data on their own infrastructure.

services: dolibarr: image: dolibarr/dolibarr:21.0.0 restart: always environment: DOLI_DB_HOST: db DOLI_DB_NAME: $DB_NAME DOLI_DB_USER: $DB_USER DOLI_DB_PASSWORD: $DB_PASSWORD DOLI_URL_ROOT: ${DOLIBARR_HOST} DOLI_ADMIN_LOGIN: admin DOLI_ADMIN_PASSWORD: $ADMIN_PASSWORD volumes: - ./dolibarr_documents:/var/www/documents - ./dolibarr_html:/var/www/html depends_on: db: condition: service_healthy container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:80' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network db: image: mariadb:10.11 restart: always environment: MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD: $DB_ROOT_PASSWORD MYSQL_DATABASE: $DB_NAME MYSQL_USER: $DB_USER MYSQL_PASSWORD: $DB_PASSWORD volumes: - ./db_data:/var/lib/mysql healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - healthcheck.sh --connect --innodb_initialized interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 start_period: 30s container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump de la base de APLICACIÓN. Clave: usa MYSQL_PWD (env), NUNCA `-p` — con `-p` y # password vacío (apps con MYSQL_RANDOM_ROOT_PASSWORD) mysql espera un prompt por stdin # y CUELGA. Prefiere el usuario de app (MYSQL_USER/PASSWORD/DATABASE); cae a root solo si # no hay usuario de app (excluye schemas de sistema, que romperían la auth al restaurar). docker compose exec -T db sh -c ' # MariaDB 11+ renombró mysqldump→mariadb-dump y mysql→mariadb: detectamos el binario. DUMP=$(command -v mysqldump || command -v mariadb-dump); CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_DATABASE:-}" ]; then export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD" exec "$DUMP" -u"$MYSQL_USER" --single-transaction --databases "$MYSQL_DATABASE" fi export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}" DBS=$("$CLI" -uroot -N -e "SHOW DATABASES" | grep -Ev "^(mysql|sys|information_schema|performance_schema)$") [ -z "$DBS" ] && { echo "REVISAR: sin bases de aplicacion para respaldar" >&2; exit 0; } exec "$DUMP" -uroot --single-transaction --databases $DBS ' > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "mysqldump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose up -d db # Readiness + restore en UN exec (credenciales DENTRO del contenedor, con MYSQL_PWD → # jamás cuelga esperando un prompt de password). Prefiere el usuario de app; cae a root. docker compose exec -T db sh -c ' CLI=$(command -v mysql || command -v mariadb) if [ -n "${MYSQL_USER:-}" ] && [ -n "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then U="$MYSQL_USER"; export MYSQL_PWD="$MYSQL_PASSWORD"; else U=root; export MYSQL_PWD="${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD:-}"; fi for i in $(seq 1 60); do "$CLI" -u"$U" -e "SELECT 1" >/dev/null 2>&1 && break; sleep 2; done exec "$CLI" -u"$U" ' < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "restore MySQL OK"
